title: DSA: Linked Lists Basics and Python Implementation
content:
### Day 34: DSA: Linked Lists Basics and Python Implementation

**Linked List** stores elements in nodes, each pointing to the next. Unlike arrays, insertion/deletion is O(1) at head.

**Node:** Contains data + pointer to next node.
**Head:** First node in the list.

**Tradeoffs:** LL has O(1) insert/delete but O(n) access. Arrays have O(1) access but O(n) insert.
practiceQuestions: How to reverse a linked list?
Time complexity of insert at head?
keyTakeaways: Linked List stores elements in nodes, each pointing to the next.
Unlike arrays, insertion/deletion is O(1) at head.
Node: Contains data + pointer to next node.
